series_description: (A) These files are used to administer and enforce the provisions of Chapter 4729 of the Ohio Revised Code. The principle purpose of these files is for the enforcement of the Pharmacy Practice Act, the laws governing the legal distribution of drugs in Ohio and provide a standard automated processing procedure for renewal of pharmacist licenses. (B) These files contain information regarding providers of continuing education programs with respect to: (1) Name and address of provider; (2) Qualifications for approval as a provider; (3) Approved provider code number.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Retain in agency until provider is no longer active. ; Electronic: Electronic: Retain on server until provider is no longer active.

series_description: (A) The purpose of these files are for reference and instruction in the administration of the agency. (B) These files contain requests for various forms supplied by the Board; correspondence regarding incomplete or improperly completed forms required by the Board; requests and correspondence regarding publications of the Board; preliminary correspondence concerning licensure with the Board (e.g. - as a pharmacist, pharmacy intern, or dangerous drug distributor). (C) These files do not contain any documents of legal importance; any correspondence dealing with board action; or any correspondence dealing with violations of the drug laws of Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year from date of correspondence. ; Electronic: Retain 1 year from date of correspondence.

series_description: (A) These files are used to administer and enforce the provisions of Chapter 4729 of the Revised Code. The principle purpose of these files is for the enforcement of the Pharmacy Practice Act, the laws governing the legal distribution of drugs in Ohio and provide a standard automated processing procedure for renewal of pharmacist licenses. (B) These files contain information regarding continuing education programs approved for continuing education credit with respect to: (1) Name and address of the sponsor; (2) General title and the date presented; (3) Type of presentation and contact time spent; (4) Amount of credit approved and accepted by the Board; (5) Approval code number assigned by the Board.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Retain in agency for 5 years ; Electronic: Retain on server for 5 years

series_description: Online investigative case management system. Includes all electronic files for cases from 2012-present. Includes subpoenas ordered by county, state or federal courts requesting State of Ohio Board of Pharmacy employees to testify on cases.
retention_statement: Electronic: To be maintained on the online system for a period of 20 years

series_description: When a facility that houses controlled substances must log the dangerous drugs that were destroyed, sign and send to the Ohio Pharmacy Board. Records include drug name, strength, quantity and who destroyed and witnessed the destruction of the drugs.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Scan or retain on site for 1 year and offsite for 4 years. ; Electronic: Electronic: retain on server for 5 years

series_description: In order for licensed facilities with the Ohio State Board of Pharmacy to destroy controlled substances they must request permission to destroy in writing. Writing includes e-mail, letter or submission to our online permission to destroy request link.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Scan or retain on site for 2 years and offsite for 3 years. ; Electronic: Electronic: retain on server for 5 years

series_description: Ohio Automated RX Reporting System (OARRS) application that was received incomplete where the applicant never provides additional information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Electronic applications to be retained on the OARRS server for a period of (1) year

series_description: Used to deposit and record revenue receipts from any known revenue source. Also used to establish departmental holding funds. Both paper and electronic files.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper receipts are retained in the fiscal office for a period of (1) year, transferred offsite and retained for (1) year ; Electronic: Electronic receipts are electronically stored through the State of Ohio OAKS FIN database indefinitely.

series_description: Used to record receipts or repayment where the original payment was in error or duplicated
retention_statement: Paper: Paper copies to be store in the fiscal office for a period of (1) year ; Electronic: Electronic copies are stored on the State of Ohio OAKS FIN database indefinitely.

series_description: ISTV''s issued to State of Ohio agencies in order to pay licensing fees owed to the Board of Pharmacy.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper copies to be retained in the fiscal office for a period of (1) year ; Electronic: Electronic copies are stored on the State of Ohio OAKS FIN database indefinitely

series_description: Applications for grant opportunities and all records concerning federal grants which document expenditure and use of federal monies.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: To be retained for a period of (7) years or until Federal audit ; Electronic: Electronic: Retained in OAKS pursuant to OBM policy.

series_description: E-Share documents outlining the seizure of property as restitution during a criminal investigation. Lists the distribution of seized properties and monies between the Board of Pharmacy and other participating law enforcement agencies.
retention_statement: Paper: To be retained in the fiscal office for a period of (1) year ; Electronic: Electronic records to be retained for 2 years on fiscal server

series_description: Check payments that are received via mail or person through the front desk.Checks are then remotely deposited digitally.
retention_statement: Paper: Copies of digitally deposited checks are retained in the fiscal office for a period of 6 months to ensure no return checks

series_description: Accounts payable ledger maintained by fiscal year.
retention_statement: Electronic: Electronic records kept on fiscal server for a period of (3) years

series_description: Invoices, bills, delivery slips and all documentation supporting the generation of a voucher payment.
retention_statement: Paper: To be maintained in the fiscal office for a period of (1) year ; Electronic: Electronic copies are stored on the State of Ohio OAKS FIN database indefinitely

series_description: Listing of long distance calls and charges made by agency personnel during a fiscal year.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Documentation tracking purchases made with petty cash funds. Also reimbursements generated for the petty cash fund.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved ; Electronic: Permenant

series_description: Refund of payments made to the Board of Pharmacy in error.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved ; Electronic: Permenant

series_description: Used to make IT purchases of materials, supplies and equipment and to engage contract of services
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved ; Electronic: Permenant

series_title: Requests for Stop or Cancellation of Warrant and Applications for Replacement Warrants
series_description: Request to invalidate a state warrant that was never received, lost or stolen, or to issue a new warrant to replace one that was lost, stolen, or never received.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Signed and approved allocation of a specific amount of 4A50 monies to be set aside for a particular vendor in the purchase of law enforcement property and services for the Compliance Department.
retention_statement: Paper: 10 years

series_description: Ledger tracking all payment card purchases by amount, vendor, product purchased and date the transaction was processed in PREP and OAKS FIN. Official transaction record is PREP/OAKS FIN. This spreadsheet is used internally for ease of searchability.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 5 Years

series_description: Initial request to generate a purchase order.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Request to set aside funds for purchase of property or services from a particular vendor.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Contractual agreement between the Board of Pharmacy and the vendor for services rendered during a set period of time.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 7 years after the expiration of contracts

series_description: Requests for office maintenance from Tenant Services.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain during the fiscal year in which the service was required and after the requested service has been fulfilled. Shred.

series_description: Daily form used by all office employees to register hours worked.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. If Federal funds are involved, retain for 3 years unless there is a finding.

series_description: Cumulative spreadsheet tracking the hours worked as submitted by all office staff for each two-week pay period.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. If Federal funds are involved, retain for 3 years unless there is a finding. ; Electronic: Permenant

series_description: All paper records related to payroll processing, including payroll register, emails documenting corrections and adjustments, and related documents. Also includes requests for manual paychecks.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: Computer file used to track complete equipment inventory.
retention_statement: Electronic: Permenant

series_description: Allows movement of funds between categories.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain onsite for (2) years ; Electronic: Permanent.

series_description: Reports sent in by pharmacies when there is a loss of a controlled substance in a pharmacy.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Scan or retain on site for 3 years. ; Electronic: Electronic: Retain on server for 3 years.

series_title: Suspicious Order, Sample Loss & Reports of Drugs Distributed
series_description: Documentation sent via email or USPS informing the Board of a suspicious order, sample loss, & reports of drugs distributed.
retention_statement: Paper: Indefinitely ; Electronic: Indefinitely

series_description: Information obtain from LEADS, OHLEG, TLO and LEXIS NEXIS which pertain to a OSBP Investigation/Case
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain on server until sent to appropriate agent

series_description: Training records for employees that are required by Law Enforcement Automated Data System or Ohio Law Enforcement Gateway before access to database.
retention_statement: Paper: On site for (8) years

series_description: Audit reports of information accessed by our agency employees through the Ohio Law Enforcement Gateway (OHLEG).
retention_statement: Paper: Signed documents/backgrounds for Board employees who access LEADS/OHLEG maintain for the duration of employment. Newsletters/TAC in-service retain 4 years from date of issuance/completion. Agency Agreement/Administrator Training maintain until change in administrator or major changes to training.

series_description: (A) These files may be used it initiate administrative actions against a registrant of the Pharmacy Board or other allied health boards (i.e. - Medical, Dental, Nursing, Veterinary Medicine, etc.); initiate criminal prosecution and/or invoke the injunction powers of the Board. (B) These files contain investigative reports and information relative to the enforcement of Chapters 29025, 3715, 3719, and 4729 of the Revised Code. These files also include complaints from private citizens and organizations, state and federal agencies, registrants, health professionals and law enforcement agencies and investigatory reports from Pharmacy Board investigators and other agencies and organizations. When a license is surrendered or revoked, wall certificates will be housed with the Enforcement Case File.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain on site for 10 years. If Board Action retain off site 10 years. If no Board Action Shred

series_description: An application from an individual from a licensed facility requesting an account for our Ohio Automated RX Reporting System (OARRS) system. Paper is obsolete (before 2017),
retention_statement: Electronic: To be stored on OARRS electronic server for the life of the account

series_title: Supporting Documents for OARRS Account Application
series_description: Description: Copies of personal and professional credentials that support an Ohio Automated RX Reporting System (OARRS) applicant’s identity for an OARRS user account and support the applicant’s identity to add delegates under their user account.
retention_statement: Electronic: To be retained on the OARRS server for the life of the account

series_title: Ohio Automated Rx Reporting System (OARRS) Exemption Correspondence
series_description: Forms signifying exemption from reporting to the Ohio Automated RX Reporting System (OARRS) database.
retention_statement: Electronic: Scan to be maintained on Ohio Automated Rx Reporting System server for 2 years

series_title: Ohio Automated Rx Reporting System (OARRS) Personal RX History Report Requests
series_description: When a member of the public wants to request a copy of their personal RX history report they must submit a notarized application. A copy of the application, report furnished and signed receipt is maintained in the Ohio Automated RX Reporting System (OARRS) department.
retention_statement: Paper: Original to be scanned to Ohio Automated Rx Reporting System server ; Electronic: Scanned copy to be retained on Ohio Automated Rx Reporting System server for 2 years

series_title: Ohio Automated Rx Reporting System (OARRS) Subpoenas
series_description: Subpoenas from County, State or Federal Courts requesting Ohio Automated Rx Reporting System (OARRS) data.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain onsite for a period of (5) years

series_title: Ohio Automated Rx Reporting System - Law Enforcement OARRS Histories
series_description: Look up history on prescribes and specific patients, requested from local law enforcement agencies. This is stored in OARRS as part of the record audit.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain on Ohio Automated Rx Reporting System server for 5 Years

series_description: (A) These reports are used to document actual payment of drug fines required by Sections 3715.73(B), 3719.21 and 4729.65 of the Revised Code and to refund fines when a conviction has been overturned by a higher court. The principle purpose of these reports is for auditor's use and to provide information regarding the extent and type of drug law violations occurring in Ohio. (B) These reports contain information with respect to: (1) Case number, court and judge; (2) Section violated, fine assessed and fine collected.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Scan or retain on site for 3 years. ; Electronic: Electronic: Retain on server for 3 years.

series_description: Audio/Video recording of Administrative Hearings and Board Meetings.
retention_statement: Electronic: Audio/Video recording of Administrative Hearings must be maintained on server and kept for a period of one year after final adjudication decision

series_description: Public rule hearings are to solicit comment new rules and those to be amended. Transcripts are made of those hearings.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain electronic copies on server for 3 years ; Paper: Retain existing paper copies for 3 years

series_title: TERMINAL DISTRIBUTOR OF DANGEROUS DRUGS FILES
series_description: (A) These files are used to administer and enforce the provisions and requirements of Chapters 3715, 3719, 4729 and 2925 of the Revised Code. The principle purpose of these files is for the enforcement of the Pharmacy Practice Act, Ohio Pure Food and Drug Act, Controlled Substances Act and Criminal Code Chapter dealing with drug crimes. (B) These files contain information on the status of Ohio terminal distributors of dangerous drugs with respect to: (1) Qualifications for registration or licensure; (2) Records of compliance with drug laws and regulations; (3) Public legal records.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Retain in agency until scanned, unless 3 years after a lapse, failed to renew or discontinuation of business ; Electronic: Electronic: Retain on server for 3 years after lapse, failed to renew or discontinuation of business

series_title: WHOLESALE DISTRIBUTOR OF DANGEROUS DRUGS FILES
series_description: (A) These files are used to administer and enforce the provisions and requirements of Chapters 3715, 3719, 4729, and 2925 of the Revised Code. The principle purpose of these files is for the enforcement of the Pharmacy Practice Act, Ohio Pure Food and Drug Act, Controlled Substances Act and Criminal Code Chapter dealing with drug crimes. (B) These files contain information on the status of Ohio wholesale distributors of dangerous drugs with respect to: (1) Qualifications for registration or licensure; (2) Records of compliance with drug laws and regulations; (3) Public Legal records.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Retain in agency until scanned, unless 3 years after a lapse, failed to renew or discontinuation of business ; Electronic: Electronic: Retain on server for 3 years after lapse, failed to renew or discontinuation of business

series_description: (A) These files are used to administer and enforce the provisions and requirements of Chapters 3715, 3719, 4729, and 2925 of the Revised Code. The principle purpose of these files is for the enforcement of the Pharmacy Practice Act, the laws governing the legal distribution of drugs in Ohio and provide a standard automated processing procedure for issuance and renewal of pharmacist licenses. (B) These files contain information on the status of Ohio Pharmacists with respect to: (1) Personal and employment records; (2) Professional records (academic and experience); (3) Public legal records.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Retain in agency until scanned, unless 3 years after a lapse or failed to renew ; Electronic: Electronic: Retain on server for 3 years after lapse or failed to renew

series_description: Spreadsheet listings of inactive or discontinued licensee files, files include pharmacist, intern, terminal distributor of dangerous drugs, wholesale distributor of dangerous drugs and case files that are sent to the Board''''s offsite storage facility.
retention_statement: Electronic: The log should retain the licensing file records for a period of (3) years until the scheduled shred date and of the files, the data may

series_description: Policy & Procedure manuals for each department, including but not limited to procedures for managing section, sample letters, supporting documents, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of administrative value, will be updated as needed ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value, will be updated as needed

series_description: Including video, PowerPoints, audio, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Until no longer of administrative value ; Electronic: Until no longer of administrative value

series_title: Access Agreements for Personal Information Databases
series_description: Access agreements for personal information databases such as Law Enforcement Automated Data System & Ohio Law Enforcement Gateway.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years after termination of account

series_description: EMPLOYEE MEDICAL RECORDS RELATING TO INJURY/ILLNESS
retention_statement: Paper: RETAIN 7 YEARS AFTER EMPLOYEE SEPARATION

series_description: Annual report of receivables, payables and licensee count for the Legislative Service Commission.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain for 3 fiscal years on Fiscal server

series_description: Documents requesting retrieval and delivery of files from offsite.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain data of all items sent to the state of Ohio Board of Pharmacy offsite storage/ on log until 3 year period is superseded and documents are destroyed.

series_description: Including, but not limited to: documents pertaining to application forms, personnel actions, benefit forms, training records (including home inspections and firearms training), workforce development, tuition reimbursement, other education assistance program records and copies or originals of other documents concerning the employee (excluding medical records), equipment inventory, state vehicle information.
retention_statement: Paper: 7 years after employee separation

series_description: AGENCY WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT PLAN
retention_statement: Electronic: Until superseded

series_description: Reports documenting the printing volume for all office copiers registered witht the Cost-Per-Copy Program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years or until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: A continuous ledger of customer checks returned to the Board of Pharmacy as unprocessable.
retention_statement: Electronic: The continuous ledger to be retained indefinitely.

series_description: Overview of Board's function
retention_statement: Paper: Until superseded

series_description: DIAGRAM OF ORGANIZATIONAL STRUCTURE
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years after superseded

series_description: OVERVIEW OF EMPLOYEE JOB RESPONSIBILITIES
retention_statement: Paper: Until superseded

series_description: Used to submit specifications for a printing job to State Printing or Copy Center and to encumber the dollars needed for the job.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years or until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Agency Strike Plan
retention_statement: Paper: Until superseded

series_description: Any official documentation explaining the absence of an employee from normal working hours, including such items as a doctors excuse or a relatives obituary notice.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. If Federal funds are involved, retain for 3 years unless there is a finding.

series_description: Financial Disclosure Statements submitted each fiscal year by board employees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain onsite for (2) years

series_description: Includes telephone messages, post-it-notes, drafts and other limited documents, which serve to convey information fo temporary importance in lieu of oral communication.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value ; Paper: Retain until no longer of administrative value

series_title: Training Manuals, Job Aids and Reference Charts
series_description: Training and reference materials produced by the board and by the State of Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_description: Includes internal correspondence (e-mail and text messages).
retention_statement: Electronic: E-mail: Retain 1 year or until no longer of administrative value. Text Messages: Delete immediately.

series_description: A form used to identify the length of time a record must be retained before final disposal.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded by a revised schedule, or until record series is no longer maintained

series_description: Increase funds either by Controlling Board action or by biennial appropriation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain onsite for (2) years ; Electronic: Retain on server for 23 years per OBM recommendation.

series_description: Includes service and operation maintenance records of licensed vehicles, warranty and guaranty statements, accident reports, repair records, mileage and useage logs, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain during ownership of vehicle

series_description: Requests to DAS Payroll to generate a manual paycheck for an employee due to an error that shorts the individual what is owed them by the Board of Pharmacy.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. If Federal funds are involved, retain for 3 years unless there is a finding.

series_description: Documentation outlining quotes and bids for goods and services.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years or until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved ; Electronic: Retain 3 years or until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: In-house electronic system used to track all check and cash payments submitted by mail or in person directly to the Board of Pharmacy.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 5 Years

series_description: Probation Reports submitted by licensed individuals and their treatment providers.
retention_statement: Paper: Duration of Probation ; Electronic: Duration of Probation

series_description: Correspondence with treatment providers or probationers about their current probation status.
retention_statement: Electronic: Delete e-mail after no longer of value or when probationer completes probation.

series_description: Including, but not limited to: information relating to the employee’s work schedule, vacation or leave, attendance records, current active disciplinary track items (one year period), last performance review, items regarding performance observations
retention_statement: Paper: Retain onsite for (2) years after seperation

series_description: EMPLOYEE RECORDS RELATED TO LABOR RELATIONS
retention_statement: Paper: 7 years after case closure

series_description: Vacancy postings & applications, resumes, civil service applications and interview questions from applicants not hired
retention_statement: Paper: 7 years after position fill date

series_description: Board Journals/Minutes - retain original copies of Board Journals/Minutes on Board of Pharmacy Website
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain Indefinitely

series_description: Requests for public records/freedom of information requests received from the public and the associated responses.
retention_statement: Electronic: Requests and responses are to be scanned and maintained on the Board''s server for a period of 3 years

series_description: These forms are used by pharmacies to report lost or stolen controlled substances as required by the DEA. Pharmacies are required to submit the Ohio Pharmacy Board a copy so our Agents can investigate loss patterns and/or diversions.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Scan or retain on site for 2 years and offsite for 3 years ; Electronic: Electronic: retain on server for 5 years

series_description: (A) These files are used to administer and enforce the provisions and requirements of Chapters 3715, 3719, 4729, and 2925 of the Revised Code. The principle purpose of these files is for the enforcement of the Pharmacy Practice Act, the laws governing the legal distribution of drugs in Ohio and provide a standard automated processing procedure for issuance and renewal of pharmacy intern licenses. (B) These files contain information on the status of Ohio Pharmacy interns with respect to: (1) Personal and employment records; (2) Professional records (academic and experience); (3) Public legal records.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Retain in agency until scanned, unless 1 year after final expiration/lapse, including all renewals, or 1 year after denial ; Electronic: Electronic: Retain on server for 3 years after lapse or failed to renew

series_description: Includes A/R, A/P, purchase order, refunds, ISTVs, etc. that are transacted in OAKS FIN. Examples include vouchers, deposits, refunds of expenditure, payment card transactions and vouchers, printing orders, budget journals and transfers, and spreadsheet journals.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: If applicable (not all transactions have associated paper records), retain until audit is completed by Auditor of State and all exceptions are resolved. ; Electronic: Electronic: Retained in OAKS pursuant to OBM policy.

series_description: FAQ sheets and general guidance, generally issued by topic or per license type
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 years from date updated or created. Review by State before disposal. ; Electronic: Retain for 3 years from date updated or created. Review by State Archives before disposal.

series_title: Federal Bureau of Investigation and Bureau of Criminal Investigations Background Check Results
series_description: Federal Bureau of Investigation and Bureau of Criminal Investigationas Background Check Results received directly from the Ohio Attorney General's office for license applications. These are received both physically and electronically.
retention_statement: Electronic: Electronic: Retain on Intranet Server for 3 years

series_description: (A) These files are used to administer and enforce the provisions and requirements of Chapters 3715, 3719, 4729, and 2925 of the Revised Code. The principle purpose of these files is for the enforcement of the Pharmacy Practice Act, the laws governing the legal distribution of drugs in Ohio and provide a standard automated processing procedure for issuance and renewal of pharmacy intern licenses. (B) These files contain information on the status of Ohio Pharmacy technicians with respect to: (1) Personal and employment records; (2) Professional records (experience and certification); (3) Public legal records.
retention_statement: Electronic: Electronic: Retain on server for 3 years after lapse or failed to renew

series_title: Home Medical Equipment Service Provider Files
series_description: A) The principle purpose of these files is for the enforcement of the laws governing the sale, delivery, installation, maintenance, replacement, or demonstration of home medical equipment to the public in Ohio and provide a standard automated processing procedure for issuance and renewal of home medical equipment service providers licenses and registrations, as set forth in Chapter 4752 of the ORC. (B) These files contain information on the status of Ohio home medical equipment service providers with respect to: (1) qualifications for registration or licensure; (2) records of compliance with drug laws and regulations; (3) Public legal records.
retention_statement: Electronic: Electronic: Retain on server for 3 years after lapse, failed to renew, or DCB

series_description: Routine inspections of licensed facilities. Housed in Matrix Inspector, electronic only.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain indefinitely

series_description: Routine inspections of licensed facilities, conducted prior to rollout of Matrix Inspector.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper: Retain in agency until scanned, unless 3 years after a lapse, failed to renew or discontinuation of business ; Electronic: Electronic: Retain on server for 3 years after lapse, failed to renew or discontinuation of business

series_description: Submission of changes to barricade and/or changes in physical structure at licensed location.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain on intranet. Delete 5 years from date of inspection related to security notifications.

series_description: Training records for new hires prior to successful conclusion of probationary period. Located in Matrix Investigator
retention_statement: Electronic: 7 years after employee separation

series_description: Contain EEO bi-annual plan, schedule, and complaints.
retention_statement: Paper: 4 years or until no longer of administrative value

series_description: Board Materials not otherwise specified in other schedules. May include but not limited to; presentation materials, presentation requests, Legislative Resolutions & Rules
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain either paper or scan for 3 years from presentation date ; Electronic: Maintain either paper or scan for 3 years from presentation date

series_description: Contains copies of citations, notices of hearings, orders, settlements and other correspondence relating to administrative case.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain Matrix Investigator Case Files Indefinitely

series_description: Materials of Board authorized Ad-Hoc Committee not otherwise specified in other schedules. May include but not limited to; presentation materials, meeting agendas and minutes, and committee recommendations.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain either paper or scan for 3 years from presentation date ; Paper: Maintain either paper or scan for 3 years from presentation date
